knowledge.html:
1. Cac loại câu điều kiện

LoaiGiải thíchCông thứcVí dụ
Conditional Sentence Type 1Câu điều kiện có thật, có thể xảy ra ở hiện tại/tương lai
If + S + V(hiện tại), S + will/won't + V(nguyen)
If it rains tomorrow, we won't go out.
Conditional Sentence Type 2Câu điều kiện không có thật không the xảy ra ở hiện tại
If + S + V(quá khứ), S + would(not) + V(nguyen)

Lưu ý: Trong câu điều kiện loại 2, chỉ dùng "to be" là "WERE"
If I had money, I would buy that car.
If I were you, I wouldn't behave like that.
Conditional Sentence Type 3Câu điều kiện không có thật không the xảy ra ở quá khứ
If + S + had + VP2, S + would(not) + have + VP2
If you had finished your homework, you wouldn't have been punished.
Conditional Sentence Mixed 3-2Câu điều kiện diễn tả tình huống trong quá khứ không thuc tệ và kết quả có thể xảy ra trong hiện tại
If + S + had + VP2 (+ yesterday, last night,...), S + would(n't) + V(nguyen) + now/right now/today/...
If you had gone to bed earlier last night, you wouldn't be tired now.

2. Một số cach diễn dat điều kiện khác

STTCấu trúcGiải thíchVí dụ
1UnlessUnless = If...not (trừ phi).
Neu mệnh đề "If" có "not", ta thế "Unless" vào vị trí của "If" và bỏ "not".
Neu mệnh đề "If" không có "not", ta vẫn thế "Unless" vào vị trí của "If" nhưng thêm hoặc bớt "not" ở mệnh đề sau.
If you don't speak loudly, he won't hear.
= Unless you speak loudly, he won't hear.
2In caseIn case + mệnh đề,... (Phong khi)
In case we stay longer, take some more money.
3ShouldIf + S + should + V, mệnh đề. (diễn tả 1 sự việc khó có thể xảy ra)
If you should see Ann, could you ask her to call me?
4Happen to VIf + S + happen to + V, mệnh đề. (nhấn mạnh sự tình cờ hoi của 1 sự việc)
If you happen to see Helen, could you ask her to call me?
5If + adjectiveIf + tính từ, mệnh đề.
If necessary, you can take a taxi.
6Were toS + were to V... (diễn tả 1 cảnh tương ở tương lai/đề nghị lịch sự).
Lưu ý: Công thức nay không áp dùng cho các động từ chỉ trạng thái nhu: know, like, remember, understand...
If the government were to cut V.A.T, prices would fall.
7Without/But forWithout But for (nếu không có...)
= If it weren't for + danh từ, mệnh đề. (DKL2)
= If it hadn't been for + danh từ, mệnh đề. (DKL3)
If you hadn't helped us, we would have been in trouble.
= But for your help, we would have been in a mess.
= If it were not for Jim, this company would be in a mess.
= Without Jim, this company would be in a mess.
8Supposing/SupposeSupposing/suppose (giả sử)
Supposing you won the football match, what would you do?
9OtherwiseOtherwise (nếu không thì).
Otherwise + real condition (điều kiện có thể thực hiện được).
Otherwise + unreal condition (điều kiện không the thực hiện được).
We must be back before midnight, otherwise I will be locked out.
He supports her finance, otherwise she couldn't conduct this survey.
I used a computer, otherwise I wouldn't have finished this report.
10Provided/as long asProvided that/providing that = as long as/so long as = on condition that + mệnh đề, mệnh đề (miễn là)
You can go home late provided that you do the homework.
11Wish/If onlyIf only/S wish + S + V(quá khứ) (ước ngược với hiện tại)
If only I had today off.
12If only (quá khứ)If only/S wish + S + V(quá khứ hoàn thành) (ước ngược với quá khứ)
They wish that they hadn't spent $500.
13It's timeIt's (high/about) time + S + V(quá khứ) (đã den lúc phải làm gi)
It's time we did our homework.
14Would ratherS + would rather + S + V(quá khứ) (muộn ai đó làm gi)
I'd rather you didn't smoke here.

exercises.question: If I were a better conversationalist, _______ to parties more often?
options: B. I would have been invited
exercises.explanation: Type 2 conditional (unreal present). Because the sentence ends with '?', this is a question, so use inverted word order: 'would I be invited?' (not 'I would be invited').

exercises.question: Should she have some more money, she _______ a better car.
exercises.explanation: 'Should she have' is an inverted form of 'If she should have' (expressing an unlikely condition). The result clause uses 'would buy'.

exercises.question: Had you been more careful, such typing mistakes _______ avoided.
exercises.explanation: 'Had you been' = 'If you had been' (Type 3). Result: 'might have been avoided'.

exercises.question: Don't worry _______ home late tonight.
exercises.explanation: After 'if' in conditional, use present tense (not will): 'if I'm'.

exercises.question: If I _______ you, I wouldn't buy that book.
exercises.explanation: Type 2: 'If I were you' - always use 'were' in unreal conditions.

exercises.question: If she _______ harder, she can pass the exam.
exercises.explanation: Type 1 (real condition): If + present simple. 'She' takes 'studies'.

exercises.question: Unless ít rains soon, a lot of plants _______.
exercises.explanation: 'Unless ít rains' = 'If ít doesn't rain' (Type 1): plants will die.

exercises.question: If I _______ an Angel, I would try to make happy all the children.
exercises.explanation: Type 2 (unreal present): If + past simple. Use 'were' for all subjects.

exercises.question: If she _______ the truth, she'd never want to see you again.
exercises.explanation: Type 2: If + past simple ('knew'), S + would + V.

exercises.question: If the wall weren't so high, he _______ ít up to take his ball down.
exercises.explanation: Type 2: If + past, S + could/would + V: 'could climb'.

exercises.question: I think he is not at home. If he _______ in, he _______ the phone.
exercises.explanation: Type 2 (unreal present): If he were in, he would answer.

exercises.question: You would study Italian if ít _______ here.
exercises.explanation: Type 2: If + past (passive: were taught).

exercises.question: I would have visited you before if there _______ a lot of people in your house.
exercises.explanation: Type 3: If + past perfect: 'if there hadn't been'.

exercises.question: You made a mistake by telling her a lie. It _______ better if you _______ to her.
options: A. would have been / hadn't lied
exercises.explanation: Type 3 (past unreal): would have been better if you hadn't lied.

exercises.question: John would take a great risk now if he _______ his money in that business last year.
exercises.explanation: Mixed conditional (3-2): If + had + PP (past), S + would + V (present result). 'Had invested'.

exercises.question: If you waited, he _______.
exercises.explanation: Type 2: If + past simple, S + would + V: 'would come'.

exercises.question: If you _______ the bell, the servant would come.
exercises.explanation: Type 2: If + past simple ('rang'), S + would + V.

exercises.question: If you press that button what _______?
exercises.explanation: Type 1 (real condition): If + present, S + will + V: 'will happen'.

exercises.question: I _______ one of my special desserts for dinner if you like.
exercises.explanation: Type 1: If + present ('if you like'), S + will + V: 'will make'.

exercises.question: The car _______ if somebody takes ít there.
exercises.explanation: Type 1 passive: will + be + PP: 'will be repaired'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Provided you kept quiet, we'll allow you to play here.
exercises.correctSentence: Provided you keep quiet, we'll allow you to play here.
exercises.explanation: Type 1 condition: 'Provided + present simple' with 'will' in result clause.

exercises.wrongSentence: We will help you realize your dream unless you don't cheat us.
exercises.correctSentence: We will help you realize your dream unless you cheat us.
exercises.explanation: 'Unless' already means 'if not', so don't add 'don't' - that creates a double negative.

exercises.wrongSentence: If Dave doesn't invite me to his birthday party today. I would feel disappointed.
exercises.correctSentence: If Dave didn't invite me to his birthday party today, I would feel disappointed.
exercises.explanation: 'Would feel' signals Type 2. The if-clause needs past simple: 'didn't invite'.

exercises.wrongSentence: If I hadn't got up earlier this morning, I would miss my flight to Seoul.
exercises.correctSentence: If I hadn't got up earlier this morning, I would have missed my flight to Seoul.
exercises.explanation: Type 3 (past unreal): If + had PP, S + would have + PP: 'would have missed'.

exercises.wrongSentence: We stay at home if ít snows tomorrow.
exercises.correctSentence: We will stay at home if ít snows tomorrow.
exercises.explanation: Type 1: result clause needs 'will': 'We will stay'.

exercises.wrongSentence: If we earned enough money, we'll buy a new hi-fi system.
exercises.correctSentence: If we earn enough money, we'll buy a new hi-fi system.
exercises.explanation: Result has 'will' (Type 1), so if-clause needs present simple: 'earn'.

exercises.wrongSentence: What would you do if you will get lost in a forest at night?
exercises.correctSentence: What would you do if you got lost in a forest at night?
exercises.explanation: Type 2: 'would' in result -> past simple in if-clause: 'if you got'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Mr. Will wouldn't have moved to London if he would found a job here last week.
exercises.correctSentence: Mr. Will wouldn't have moved to London if he had found a job here last week.
exercises.explanation: Type 3: if-clause uses 'had + PP': 'if he had found'.

exercises.wrongSentence: What do you do if you won the first prize of the lottery?
exercises.correctSentence: What would you do if you won the first prize of the lottery?
exercises.explanation: Type 2 ('won' in if-clause): result needs 'would': 'would you do'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Would people be able to fly if they have feathers instead of hair?
exercises.correctSentence: Would people be able to fly if they had feathers instead of hair?
exercises.explanation: Type 2: 'would' in result -> past simple in if-clause: 'if they had'.

exercises.wrongSentence: I would do ít if I could, but I can't so I wouldn't even try.
exercises.correctSentence: I would do ít if I could, but I can't so I won't even try.
exercises.explanation: The second part is a real statement about the present: 'I can't, so I won't try'.

exercises.wrongSentence: If Peter had been more careful, he wouldn't break the camera I lent him.
exercises.correctSentence: If Peter had been more careful, he wouldn't have broken the camera I lent him.
exercises.explanation: Type 3: If + had PP, S + wouldn't have + PP.

exercises.wrongSentence: Roger wouldn't have made such a lot of mistakes if he hasn't been so tired.
exercises.correctSentence: Roger wouldn't have made such a lot of mistakes if he hadn't been so tired.
exercises.explanation: Type 3: if-clause needs past perfect: 'hadn't been'.

exercises.wrongSentence: How nice it would be for our parents if we could built a house for them.
exercises.correctSentence: How nice it would be for our parents if we could build a house for them.
exercises.explanation: After 'could' (modal), use bare infinitive: 'could build'.

exercises.wrongSentence: If your son were old enough, he could be able to take the competition next week.
exercises.correctSentence: If your son were old enough, he would be able to take the competition next week.
exercises.explanation: 'Could' and 'be able to' are redundant together. Use 'would be able to'.

exercises.wrongSentence: I would have called you if I could have known your number.
exercises.correctSentence: I would have called you if I had known your number.
exercises.explanation: Type 3 if-clause: If + had + PP. 'Could have known' is wrong in the if-clause.

exercises.wrongSentence: If I knew Linda's address, I would sent her a Christmas card now.
exercises.correctSentence: If I knew Linda's address, I would send her a Christmas card now.
exercises.explanation: After 'would', use bare infinitive: 'would send', not 'would sent'.

exercises.wrongSentence: What does Jane do if she fails the university entrance examination?
exercises.correctSentence: What will Jane do if she fails the university entrance examination?
exercises.explanation: Type 1: result clause with 'will': 'What will Jane do?'

exercises.wrongSentence: If John's parents know French, they could help him with his project.
exercises.correctSentence: If John's parents knew French, they could help him with his project.
exercises.explanation: Type 2: 'could' in result -> past simple in if-clause: 'knew'.

exercises.wrongSentence: If Laura apologized to me for her bad behaviour yesterday, I would have forgiven her.
exercises.correctSentence: If Laura had apologized to me for her bad behaviour yesterday, I would have forgiven her.
exercises.explanation: Type 3 (past unreal): if-clause needs 'had + PP': 'had apologized'.

exercises.original: They weren't listening carefully so they didn't understand what she was saying.
exercises.answer: They might have understood what she was saying if they had been listening carefully.
acceptedAnswers: They might have understood what she was saying if they had been listening carefully.
They might have understood what she was saying if they had been listening carefully
They might have understood what she was saying if they had listened carefully.
They might have understood what she was saying if they had listened carefully
exercises.explanation: Type 3: might have + PP + if + had + PP.

exercises.original: You took me to your friend's party and I met Hung.
exercises.answer: If you hadn't taken me to your friend's party, I wouldn't have met Hung.
acceptedAnswers: If you hadn't taken me to your friend's party, I wouldn't have met Hung.
If you hadn't taken me to your friend's party, I wouldn't have met Hung
exercises.explanation: Type 3 (past unreal): If + hadn't + PP, S + wouldn't have + PP.

exercises.original: If you don't wear a helmet, you can't ride your bike.
exercises.answer: Unless you wear a helmet, you can't ride your bike.
acceptedAnswers: Unless you wear a helmet, you can't ride your bike.
Unless you wear a helmet, you can't ride your bike
exercises.explanation: 'Unless' = 'If...not'. Remove 'don't' when replacing 'If' with 'Unless'.

exercises.original: I would visit my grandpa, but ít's raining.
exercises.answer: If ít weren't raining, I would visit my grandpa.
acceptedAnswers: If ít weren't raining, I would visit my grandpa.
If ít weren't raining, I would visit my grandpa
If it wasn't raining, I would visit my grandpa.
If it wasn't raining, I would visit my grandpa
exercises.explanation: Type 2: If + past (weren't raining), S + would + V.

exercises.original: It's a pity that I don't have enough money, so I can't buy that dress.
exercises.answer: If I had enough money, I could buy that dress.
acceptedAnswers: If I had enough money, I could buy that dress.
If I had enough money, I could buy that dress
If I had enough money, I would buy that dress.
If I had enough money, I would buy that dress
exercises.explanation: Type 2 (unreal present wish): If + past, S + could/would + V.

exercises.original: Sue didn't miss the train because I woke her up in time.
exercises.answer: If I hadn't woken her up in time, Sue would have missed the train.
acceptedAnswers: If I hadn't woken her up in time, Sue would have missed the train.
If I hadn't woken her up in time, Sue would have missed the train
exercises.explanation: Type 3: If + hadn't PP, S + would have PP.

exercises.original: Dan got so angry because we were an hour late.
exercises.answer: Dan wouldn't have got so angry if we hadn't been an hour late.
acceptedAnswers: Dan wouldn't have got so angry if we hadn't been an hour late.
Dan wouldn't have got so angry if we hadn't been an hour late
Dan wouldn't have gotten so angry if we hadn't been an hour late.
Dan wouldn't have gotten so angry if we hadn't been an hour late
exercises.explanation: Type 3: S + wouldn't have PP + if + hadn't PP. Both 'got' and 'gotten' are accepted as past participles of 'get'.

exercises.original: Julia didn't take her umbrella yesterday, so she got wet.
exercises.answer: If Julia had taken her umbrella yesterday, she wouldn't have got wet.
acceptedAnswers: If Julia had taken her umbrella yesterday, she wouldn't have got wet.
If Julia had taken her umbrella yesterday, she wouldn't have got wet
If Julia had taken her umbrella yesterday, she wouldn't have gotten wet.
If Julia had taken her umbrella yesterday, she wouldn't have gotten wet
exercises.explanation: Type 3: If + had PP, S + wouldn't have PP.

exercises.original: Study hard or you'll fail the exam.
exercises.answer: If you don't study hard, you'll fail the exam.
acceptedAnswers: If you don't study hard, you'll fail the exam.
If you don't study hard, you'll fail the exam
If you don't study hard, you will fail the exam.
If you don't study hard, you will fail the exam
exercises.explanation: Type 1: 'or' = 'if...not': If you don't study hard, you'll fail.

exercises.original: I don't see you very often because you live so far.
exercises.answer: If you didn't live so far, I would see you more often.
acceptedAnswers: If you didn't live so far, I would see you more often.
If you didn't live so far, I would see you more often
If you didn't live so far away, I would see you more often.
If you didn't live so far away, I would see you more often
exercises.explanation: Type 2 (unreal present): If + didn't V, S + would + V.
